authorization: Authorize s10 D1 MNQ+MGC Step 02b fetch script dataframe-normalization patch.

problem_statement.fail_closed_message: Output DataFrame missing required columns ['ts_event']; fail-closed.
problem_statement.root_cause: Required-column check ran BEFORE the reset_index() recovery, so the fail-closed fired immediately when ts_event was an index rather than a column. Recovery logic was unreachable.

patch_summary.name: _normalize_dbn_dataframe
patch_summary.behavior: Case 1: ts_event already a column -> return unchanged. Case 2: index has a name; reset_index surfaces it as a column. Case 3: index unnamed; reset_index produced 'index' column; safe rename only if 'index' dtype is datetime64. Fallback: return df2 and let caller's required-column check fail-closed.
defensive_properties: conservative renaming only when dtype is datetime-like
no row content access (only column names, index name, dtype check)

patch_summary.name: _write_csv_via_pandas
patch_summary.modification: Call _normalize_dbn_dataframe(df) BEFORE the required-column check. Update fail-closed message to mention 'after normalization'. Remove the now-redundant inline reset_index logic.
Replace datetime.utcnow().isoformat() + "Z" with datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat() in manifest fetch_run_utc.
Add timezone to 'from datetime import date, datetime, timezone'.

Added section 0 (Rerun-after-patch quick reference). Updated section 4 to mention DataFrame normalization in the flow. Added section 4.1 documenting expected non-fatal warnings, including the BentoWarning reduced-quality-days handling protocol (captured and forwarded to Step 02c raw-data audit; not handled by Step 02b).

reduced_quality_day_handling.script_action_post_patch: none (no date-range modification, no day exclusion)
reduced_quality_day_handling.runbook_action_post_patch: operator captures warning text safely and forwards to Step 02c raw-data audit
reduced_quality_day_handling.rationale: Step 02b is fetch-only by sealed scope. Reduced-quality flags are an audit concern at Step 02c. The script preserves full sealed-spec fidelity (date range unchanged; no day exclusions) and defers all data-quality decisions to the Step 02c audit phase.

next_step_authorization_paths.label: operator_reruns_patched_fetch_in_local_shell
next_step_authorization_paths.preflight_check_for_operator: Verify data/s10_d1_mnq_mgc_databento_long_history/raw/ is empty or absent before rerun; the first failed run wrote no partial CSV but if any is present, delete locally before rerun.

next_step_authorization_paths.label: after_safe_paste_back_next_authorization
next_step_authorization_paths.phrase: Authorize s10 D1 MNQ+MGC Step 02c raw-data audit only.
next_step_authorization_paths.scope: local-only audit; no Databento call; no network; consumes the manifest JSON and the operator-captured reduced-quality-day warning text to validate row counts, sha256 anchors, date-window coverage, required-column presence, and DR9 thresholds (0.95 pct expected trading days; 0.30 max consecutive abs log return; 5 max missing observations; 5 max consecutive violation threshold).

next_step_authorization_paths.label: if_rerun_fails_closed_again
actions: Do NOT modify the script without a fresh patch authorization
Do NOT modify the runbook without authorization
Paste back safe summary line + exit code + any non-fatal warning text
Controller assesses whether second patch is warranted OR whether candidate should be parked under data-availability finding

seal_method: sha256(json.dumps(body, sort_keys=True, separators=(',',':'), ensure_ascii=False)) excluding report_seal_sha256+seal_method
